channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) has launched a new ecosystem of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This expansion enhances the capabilities of AI applications in real-world scenarios.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring sessions on agentic AI and Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework that enhances GPU and NPU acceleration for TensorFlow applications. This update aims to improve efficiency and security within the TensorFlow ecosystem.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly interactive session aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and productivity tools.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to enhance collaboration between developers and AI.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce manual coding efforts. These tools help developers by completing code and generating summaries.

item.title: The largest orbital compute cluster is open for business
item.description: Kepler Communications has launched the largest orbital compute cluster, featuring 40 GPUs in Earth orbit, with Sophia Space as its first customer. This innovation marks a significant step in space-based computing.

item.title: IT manager approved downtime over lunch, but made a meal of it
item.description: An IT manager's lunch downtime turned problematic due to defective hardware, leading to humorous reflections on workplace mishaps. This anecdote highlights the unpredictability of tech management.

item.title: KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've used in months - but there's a catch
item.description: KDE Linux is praised as a superior version of Plasma, offering a unique experience compared to other Linux distributions. However, it comes with some limitations that users should consider.

item.title: China wants AI to prepare school lessons and mark homework
item.description: China's National Data Administration has unveiled an action plan to integrate AI in education, focusing on upskilling citizens for effective technology use. This initiative aims to enhance educational outcomes and prepare students for a tech-driven future.

item.title: After trying these boomless headphones in the office, I'm feeling hopeful for the future of work tech
item.description: The Jabra Evolve3 75 headphones have been reviewed positively for their performance in hybrid work environments, highlighting their effectiveness for business use. Users report improved sound quality and comfort during extended use.

item.title: LG C6 vs. LG C5: Why the 2025 model is still the smarter OLED TV model buy for me
item.description: The LG C6 OLED TV is compared to its predecessor, the C5, with discussions on whether the upgrades justify the cost. The review suggests that the C6 remains a strong choice for buyers looking for high-quality displays.

item.title: How I disabled 'fast startup' on my Windows 11 laptop to stop overnight battery drain
item.description: A guide on disabling 'fast startup' on Windows 11 laptops to prevent battery drain overnight. The article explains the benefits of this adjustment for better battery management.

item.title: YouTube Premium is getting a price increase in June - but you can save $32 with one change
item.description: YouTube Premium will see a price increase in June, but users can save money by adjusting their plans. The article outlines the new pricing structure and savings options.

item.title: Linux 7.0 debuts as Linus Torvalds ponders AI's bug-finding powers and their impact on release process
item.description: The release of Linux 7.0 includes official Rust support and improvements for older CPUs. Linus Torvalds discusses the potential role of AI in identifying bugs during the release process.

item.title: Anthropic's mysterious Mythos AI threatens to upend the infosec world
item.description: Anthropic has launched Mythos, an AI model designed to identify and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities, raising concerns and excitement in the infosec community. Its capabilities could significantly impact cybersecurity practices.

item.title: Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic’s Mythos model
item.description: Reports suggest that Trump officials may be encouraging banks to test Anthropic's Mythos model, despite concerns from the Department of Defense about supply-chain risks associated with the company.

item.title: Apple reportedly testing four designs for upcoming smart glasses
item.description: Apple is reportedly testing four designs for its upcoming smart glasses, moving away from earlier ambitious plans for a range of mixed and augmented reality devices. This reflects a more cautious approach in product development.

item.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
item.description: A glossary has been created to explain common AI terms, addressing the confusion arising from the rapid growth of AI technology. This resource aims to help users understand key concepts in the field.

item.title: At the HumanX conference, everyone was talking about Claude
item.description: Anthropic gained significant attention at the HumanX conference in San Francisco, showcasing its advancements in AI technology. This highlights the growing influence of AI companies in major tech events.

item.title: I vibe coded a feed reading web app. It was enlightening and uncomfortable
item.description: The author reflects on the transformative impact of AI-assisted software development, expressing mixed feelings about its effectiveness and permanence in the industry. This underscores the ongoing evolution of coding practices.

item.title: 30 years later, I returned to Enlightenment Linux to test the Elive beta - and it's much better
item.description: A user reviews the Elive beta of Enlightenment Linux, noting improvements in the old-school desktop experience despite some missing features. This indicates a revival of interest in classic computing environments.

item.title: Growing void between enterprise and frontier AI puts open weights models in the spotlight
item.description: The gap between enterprise and frontier AI is leading to increased interest in open weights models, which are more accessible and cost-effective for businesses. This shift could democratize AI technology further.

item.title: Here's my favorite email trick for cleaning up inbox clutter - automatically
item.description: An article provides a simple email trick to declutter inboxes by utilizing built-in features in most email solutions. This practical advice can help users manage their digital communications more effectively.

item.title: Apple update looks like Czech mate for locked-out iPhone user
item.description: A university student faces issues accessing their iPhone due to a missing character from the Czech keyboard, highlighting potential problems with device compatibility and user accessibility. This raises concerns about global usability.

item.title: The AI code wars are heating up
item.description: The newsletter discusses the escalating competition in AI coding, particularly focusing on the rise of vibe coding. This reflects the rapid evolution and commercialization of AI in software development.

item.title: Sitdown Sunday: Can you get a refund for a haunted house?
item.description: This article compiles some of the week's best longreads, providing readers with a selection of engaging stories. It serves as a resource for those seeking in-depth content.

item.title: vaultak-mcp 1.0.0
item.description: The Vaultak MCP Server is introduced as an AI agent runtime security solution for Claude, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible assistants. This development highlights the need for enhanced security in AI applications.

item.title: vaultak-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: Vaultak MCP has been added to PyPI, expanding its availability as an AI agent runtime security solution for various assistants. This move enhances the accessibility of security tools for developers.

item.title: Nike Air Force 1 Low “Cow Print” Pack Releases Summer 2026
item.description: Nike is set to release the Air Force 1 Low 'Cow Print' Pack in Summer 2026, adding to its diverse sneaker lineup. This release signifies Nike's ongoing innovation in sneaker designs.

item.title: pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260412
item.description: The pyg-nightly 2.8.0.dev20260412 is a new version of a Graph Neural Network library for PyTorch, aimed at enhancing AI model development. This update reflects ongoing advancements in AI frameworks.

item.title: Alphabet Stock 2026: Buy or Sell GOOGL as AI Momentum and $180B CapEx Bet Collide Ahead of Q1 Earnings
item.description: Alphabet Inc. shares are trading around $317, with analysts maintaining a 'Buy' rating due to strong AI-driven growth in Google Cloud and Search. This positions Alphabet favorably ahead of its Q1 earnings report.

item.title: gptperoz 0.2.2
item.description: gptperoz 0.2.2 is introduced as a simple alternative for training AI using PyTorch in just three lines of code. This simplicity could attract more developers to AI training.

item.title: mcp-bastion-azure 0.1.1
item.description: The mcp-bastion-azure 0.1.1 is a security middleware for Azure OpenAI Service, providing protections against prompt injection, PII leaks, and rate limits. This is crucial for maintaining data security in AI applications.

item.title: mcp-bastion-huggingface 0.1.1
item.description: The mcp-bastion-huggingface 0.1.1 is a security middleware designed for Hugging Face Inference, offering similar protections as its Azure counterpart. This reflects the increasing need for secure AI frameworks.

item.title: mcp-bastion-deepseek 0.1.1
item.description: The mcp-bastion-deepseek 0.1.1 middleware provides security for DeepSeek AI, focusing on prompt injection and PII protection. This highlights the importance of security in AI services.

item.title: Asymco One Office Hours Live Q&A with Horace Dediu: April 8, 2026 → Full Recording & Transcript
item.description: In a live Q&A, Horace Dediu discussed various Apple topics, including geopolitics, financial growth, and a shift in AI narratives. This conversation sheds light on Apple's strategic direction.

item.title: codex-switch 0.2.0
item.description: codex-switch 0.2.0 allows users to manage multiple OpenAI Codex CLI accounts, facilitating easier account switching and quota management. This tool is beneficial for developers using Codex.

item.title: gptperoz 0.2.0
item.description: gptperoz 0.2.0 is another version of the simple PyTorch alternative for training AI, emphasizing ease of use. This could attract more users to AI development.

item.title: mcp-bastion-deepseek added to PyPI
item.description: MCP-Bastion security middleware has been added to PyPI for DeepSeek AI, providing protections against prompt injection, PII, and rate-limiting issues.

item.title: mcp-bastion-huggingface added to PyPI
item.description: MCP-Bastion security middleware is now available on PyPI for Hugging Face Inference, focusing on prompt injection, PII, and rate-limit protection.

item.title: agent-episodic-memory added to PyPI
item.description: Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) middleware has been introduced for LangChain, enabling self-improving playbooks based on research from ICLR 2026.

item.title: agent-episodic-memory 0.1.0
item.description: The release of Agentic Context Engineering (ACE) middleware version 0.1.0 for LangChain offers self-improving playbooks inspired by ICLR 2026 research.

item.title: Judge Orders Pentagon to Grant Full Press Access, Citing First Amendment
item.description: A federal judge has mandated the Pentagon to grant full press access to journalists, ruling that previous denials violated First Amendment rights.

item.title: The Rise and Sudden Fall of OpenAI’s Sora
item.description: OpenAI has decided to shut down its AI video generation app, Sora, just 103 days after launch, raising questions about its strategic direction.

item.title: mcp-bastion-azure added to PyPI
item.description: MCP-Bastion security middleware has been added to PyPI for Azure OpenAI Service, focusing on prompt injection, PII, and rate-limit protection.

item.title: mcp-bastion-mistral 0.1.0
item.description: MCP-Bastion security middleware version 0.1.0 has been released for Mistral AI, providing protections against prompt injection, PII, and rate-limiting.

item.title: mcp-bastion-mistral added to PyPI
item.description: MCP-Bastion security middleware is now available on PyPI for Mistral AI, offering protections against prompt injection, PII, and rate-limit issues.

item.title: evalguard-python added to PyPI
item.description: EvalGuard has launched a Python SDK to evaluate, red-team, and guard LLM applications, featuring easy framework integrations.
